entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: The crop species Pennisetum glaucum studied for water transport responses.

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION RESPONSE
entities.description: The response of the plant's transpiration rate to changes in vapour pressure deficit.

entities.name: VAPOUR PRESSURE DEFICIT
entities.description: The difference between the amount of moisture in the air and how much moisture the air can hold when it is saturated.

entities.name: SYMPLASTIC WATER TRANSPORT PATHWAY
entities.description: Water transport through the cells via aquaporin-facilitated transport.

entities.name: APOPLASTIC WATER TRANSPORT PATHWAY
entities.description: Water transport through the spaces between cells.

entities.name: HYDRAULIC CONDUCTIVITY
entities.description: The ease with which water can move through plant tissues.

entities.name: PRLT 2/89/33
entities.description: A low transpiration rate genotype of pearl millet.

entities.name: H77/833-2
entities.description: A high transpiration rate genotype of pearl millet.

entities.name: HYDROPONICS
entities.description: A method of growing plants without soil used to test transpiration.

entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: The growth medium used to test transpiration responses.

entities.name: DETACHED LEAF ANALYSIS
entities.description: The process of measuring transpiration response in leaves separated from the plant.

entities.name: INHIBITOR TREATMENT
entities.description: The application of chemicals like AgNO3, H2O2, or copper precipitates to block water pathways.

entities.name: AQUAPORINS
entities.description: Water channel proteins that facilitate symplastic water transport.

entities.name: ROOT HYDRAULIC CONDUCTIVITY
entities.description: The conductivity of water specifically through the root system.

entities.name: EVAPORATIVE DEMAND
entities.description: The atmospheric demand for water, which influences hydraulic conductivity.

relationships.description: depends more on under high demand
